title: Field Management on Oracle
text: Field Management on Oracle Mandatory fields are created with the Not Null constraint, unless they are part of the primary key. This is because Primary Key fields in Oracle implicitly have the Not Null constraint. Segmented Interfaces Uniface uses segmented I/O provided by Oracle when reading a field with a segmented interface. The Oracle connector implements BLOBs as CLOB and BLOB data types, which are also written to the database in segments. CAUTION: If you use segmented fields on a platform more restrictive than a 32-bit virtual memory operating system, you can encounter severe restrictions when attempting to update or insert a record. Furthermore, updating or inserting large segmented fields can cause other activities on your system to fail. In this case, consider using the Uniface proprietary variable-length techniques (overflow tables) instead of the segmented field interface. To do so, set the disable segmented fields connector option. If the amount of memory required to write the segmented field cannot be allocated, the following error can occur: Oracle connector Error[-4]: Dynamic memory allocation failed. Copy Increasing available memory can solve the problem. For example, with virtual memory operating systems, you can increase storage available for paging and swapping. Was this topic helpful?
